channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Webb telescope detects carbon dioxide, hydrogen peroxide on Pluto’s Charon for the first time
item.description: Observations by the James Webb Space Telescope are giving scientists a fuller understanding about the composition and evolution of Pluto’s moon Charon, the largest moon orbiting any of our solar system’s dwarf planets.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Epic Games is suing Google and Samsung for this phone setting
item.description: Video game maker Epic Games sued Google and Samsung on Monday, accusing the tech companies of coordinating to block third-party competition in application distribution on Samsung devices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: IBM expands Nvidia GPU options for cloud customers
item.description: IBM is offering expanded access to Nvidia GPUs on IBM Cloud to help enterprise customers advance their AI implementations, including large language model (LLM) training.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: ONCD’s Harry Wingo on lowering barriers to good-paying cyber jobs
item.description: The Office of the National Cyber Director wants to fill thousands of open federal jobs under its Service for America initiative. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: ONCD’s Harry Wingo on lowering barriers to good-paying cyber jobs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: US modifies AI chip export rules, boosting Middle East access
item.description: In a move aimed at facilitating the global development and supply of artificial intelligence (AI), the US Commerce Department has brought a new policy that will ease export restrictions for advanced AI chips to data centers in the Middle East and Central Asia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to use Loop components in Microsoft 365 apps
item.description: Microsoft’s ambitious collaboration app, Microsoft Loop, includes shared workspaces as well as portable content snippets called Loop components. These components can be shared and embedded in multiple Microsoft 365 apps.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The battle cry of 2025: Do cloud local!
item.description: Microclouds can have many names and offer many features. They can be hosted by a regional cloud provider or specialize in a specific use case, such as the rise of GPU cloud providers focusing on the exploding AI space.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Verizon customers report major outage on mobile network
item.description: Tens of thousands of Verizon users reported outages for the wireless carrier’s services across the U.S. on Monday, with Chicago and Indianapolis among the hardest-hit cities and some iPhone users stuck in “SOS” mode. According to tracking website Downdetector.com, the outage began at around 9:30 a.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok owner ByteDance may use a domestic source to develop a new AI model
item.description: TikTok’s Chinese parent ByteDance plans to develop an AI model trained primarily with chips from compatriot Huawei Technologies, said three people familiar with the matter, as U.S. curbs turn the social media giant homeward in search of chips.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Waymo self-driving car interrupts Vice President Harris’ motorcade in San Francisco
item.description: Harris, the Democratic presidential nominee, was in the city staying overnight Friday at the Fairmont Hotel before a campaign fundraiser at the San Francisco Palace of Fine Arts Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kristin J. Bender, The Mercury News

item.title: Arkansas sues YouTube over claims that the site is fueling a mental health crisis
item.description: Arkansas has sued YouTube and its parent company Alphabet, saying the video-sharing platform is deliberately addictive and fueling a mental health crisis among youth.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Fortnite maker accuses Samsung, Google of colluding to block app store competitors
item.description: Fortnite maker Epic Games filed a lawsuit Monday accusing Google and Samsung, the world’s largest Android maker, of conspiring to block third-party competitors of the Google Play Store.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Making use of command history on Linux
item.description: Command history on Linux allows you to review and repeat previous commands with little effort. It is built into the bash shell (included in the shell executable).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Arm, Intel, and the vengeance of Newton
item.description: Arm’s attempt to purchase part of Intel’s computer chip business may have been declined, but it once again demonstrates the important role played in the evolution of today’s tech by Apple and its handheld Newton.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Thousands of Verizon users report outages
item.description: Thousands of Verizon users across the country were experiencing service outages Monday morning. More than 100,000 Verizon users had reported outages as of midday Monday, according to Downdetector. The outage tracker’s map showed hotspots near Tampa, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk: Voting for Trump 'only way' to 'save' democracy
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said Sunday that former President Trump must win the election in November if America wants to preserve its democracy. “Very few Americans realize that, if Trump is NOT elected, this will be the last election,” Musk posted on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft Adds New Safety Tools to Azure AI Studio
item.description: Microsoft adds new safety tools to Azure AI Studio, including the ability to detect prompt injection attacks and test for protected material.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IT gains open-source tools to manage iPhones, iPads, and Macs
item.description: If you happen to be, or know, an open-source advocate who’s fallen into Apple IT management, here’s some welcome news: all-remote, open-source device management platform Fleet has introduced iOS device support to supplement its existing Mac, Windows, and Linux device management capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon's $4 billion partnership with AI startup Anthropic gets UK competition clearance
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Friday that it's clearing Amazon's partnership with artificial intelligence company Anthropic because the $4 billion deal didn't qualify for further scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cloud Repatriation: A Nuanced Approach in 2025
item.description: Cloud repatriation, public cloud providers, cost optimization
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kristen Bell is a Meta AI voice after criticizing company in post
item.description: After posting an "opt out" declaration on Instagram, the actress has now signed with Meta to use her voice in its AI chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: X requests it be reinstated in Brazil after complying with judge's orders, sources say
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — In the high-stakes showdown between the world’s richest man and a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, Elon Musk blinked. Musk’s social media site X has complied with Alexandre de Moraes’ orders and requested its service be reestablished in the country, two sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tabnine AI agents generate, validate code for Jira issues
item.description: AI coding assistant Tabnine has added two AI agents that integrate with the Atlassian Jira project management platform, one for generating code from requirements outlined in Jira issues and one for validating code for those requirements.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Police are probing apparent cyber vandalism on Wi-Fi networks at UK train stations
item.description: LONDON (AP) — U.K. transport officials and police said Thursday they are investigating a “cyber-security incident” after users of public Wi-Fi networks at the country’s biggest railway stations reported being shown anti-Muslim messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: One Tech Tip: Using Instagram's new teen account settings to supervise young users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Worried about your teen's Instagram use? Faced with growing pressure to do more to stop online harm, the social media platform has rolled out a major new update to protect young people and give parents more control.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI Chief Technology Officer Mira Murati and 2 other execs are leaving the ChatGPT maker
item.description: A high-ranking executive at OpenAI who served a few days as its interim CEO during a period of turmoil last year said she's leaving the artificial intelligence company.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NTSB engineer says carbon fiber hull from submersible showed signs of flaws
item.description: The carbon fiber hull of the experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreckage of the Titanic had imperfections dating to the manufacturing process and behaved differently after a loud bang was heard on one of the dives the year before the tragedy, an engineer with the National Transpo
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: X releases its first transparency report since Elon Musk's takeover
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Social media platform X on Wednesday published its first transparency report since the company was purchased by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: CrowdStrike executive apologizes to Congress for July global tech outage
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An executive at cybersecurity company CrowdStrike apologized in testimony to Congress for sparking a global technology outage over the summer. “We let our customers down,” said Adam Meyers, who leads CrowdStrike's threat intelligence division, in a hearing before a U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
